rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The markdown documents an explicit decision table, recommendations, and record-level flags. However the workbook could not be parsed for formula verification (openpyxl unavailable) and the markdown contains numeric inconsistencies (baseline control_gap_pct average reported as 71.5% while record-level values imply ~101.9%). Cost accounting and intermediate formulae are not independently verifiable from previews. Therefore reproducibility and numeric traceability are not confirmed.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open and supply a parseable workbook or include an exported worksheet showing cell formulas and ranges (or provide an explicit sheet/cell reference map) so intermediate calculations (per-record metric computations, cost aggregation, sensitivity shifts) are directly inspectable. Ensure explicit line-item cost calculations are present in the workbook and reference the embedded-packet R1..R4 or limit_* inputs in cell comments or adjacent citation columns.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Required artifacts are present. controls_setpoint.md preview contains selected setpoints, a baseline/mitigated/sensitivity decision table, a needs_review label for R4, validation checks, and an expert note explaining the zero incremental implementation cost temporary stand-in. ActualOutputs metadata reports a mitigated improvement of ~54.97% and implementation_cost_usd = 0 with within_budget=true, satisfying the budget (<= 70000 USD) and improvement (>= 10%) success criteria. However, the workbook (greenhouse_climate_tradeoff.xlsx) could not be inspected for preserved formulas/executable logic (openpyxl unavailable in the preview environment), so reproducibility of intermediate calculations could not be independently verified.
